question.text: An InvIT wants to issue units through private placement. Draft placement memorandum must be filed with SEBI and stock exchange(s) how many days prior to opening of the issue? And within what period must SEBI issue its observations?

answer.text: Not less than 30 days prior; SEBI observations within 15 days of filing
Not less than 15 days prior; SEBI observations within 30 days of filing
Not less than 5 working days prior; SEBI observations within 30 days of filing
Not less than 21 days prior; SEBI observations within 21 days of filing

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, for private placement, the draft placement memorandum must be filed through a merchant banker not less than thirty days prior to opening of the issue. SEBI shall issue observations, if any, within 15 days of filing. **The Trap:** 5 working days is the timeline for filing the offer document (not draft) with stock exchanges and SEBI before opening a public issue. The private placement process has different timelines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Private placement timeline: draft memorandum filed 30 days before issue, SEBI observations within 15 days, issue must open within 3 months of in-principle approval, listing within 6 working days of close.
